_about: v2 PyTorch + MPC reference results. Used by 02_unet_training.ipynb's Cell 10 to render a side-by-side comparison vs the GEE-port training run. All numbers extracted from ../s1s2-translator/data/runs/v2_diverse_heath/.

variance_retention_driver_bands_patch_sp._about: v2's central methodological refinement: pred-std / patch-truth-std, computed per Ashdown patch and averaged. Driver bands = B04, B08, B11, B12. Pass bracket = [75, 105]; below = collapse, above = noisy.
variance_retention_driver_bands_patch_sp.verdict_unet: FAIL (below 75% bracket; documented architectural limitation, but 3.3x baseline still establishes the U-Net's benefit)

tolerance_for_phase_2_comparison._about: From the Phase 2 kickoff prompt: deviations beyond these brackets are stop-and-investigate.

footnotes.note: GEE-port Ashdown produces 105 patches versus v2's 76 (+38 percent). Two compounding causes, neither a bug: (1) the GEE port's random-origin sampler with patch_overlap_pixels=128 fits more origins per scene in the 5 km Ashdown buffer than v2's deterministic grid; (2) GEE's COPERNICUS/S2_SR_HARMONIZED indexing yields slightly more candidate scenes than MPC's sentinel-2-l2a for the same windows. Phase 2 should compute Ashdown metrics on the full 105-patch test set and footnote the divergence in the comparison table; do not subsample to match v2. See docs/methodology_divergences.md section 2.

GEE-calibrated gamma-naught runs ~1.5 dB below MPC RTC across all validation samples uniformly (Vollrath/Mullissa volumetric model vs ESA range-Doppler). Internally consistent for training; see docs/calibration_methodology.md and docs/methodology_divergences.md section 1.

10 None-cloud-check warnings on T30UWB tile, all for Beaulieu Heath which straddles the T30UWB/T30UXB MGRS boundary. Diagnostic confirms zero recoverable patches: each scene covers 3.5-25.1% of Beaulieu's bbox and has too few SCL pixels for even one 256x256 patch. New Forest's 20.5% share (vs v2's 23.9%) is meteorology + AOI-size variance, not a tile-boundary artefact. See docs/methodology_divergences.md section 3.
